title: [FIRRTL] Invalid clock values crashing canonicalization
body:
```
circuit X:
  module X:
    output c: Clock
    wire w: Clock
    w is invalid
    c <= w
```

```
 #9 0x00000000006dc0a9 circt::firrtl::IntType::getWidthOrSentinel() /scratch/aryoung/circt/build-release/../include/circt/Dialect/FIRRTL/FIRRTLTypes.h:225:27
#10 0x00000000006dc0a9 getIntZerosAttr(mlir::Type) /scratch/aryoung/circt/lib/Dialect/FIRRTL/FIRRTLFolds.cpp:53:62
#11 0x00000000006e9d7d canonicalizeSingleSetConnect /scratch/aryoung/circt/lib/Dialect/FIRRTL/FIRRTLFolds.cpp:1435:67
#12 0x00000000006e9d7d circt::firrtl::ConnectOp::canonicalize(circt::firrtl::ConnectOp, mlir::PatternRewriter&) /scratch/aryoung/circt/lib/Dialect/FIRRTL/FIRRTLFolds.cpp:1461:16
```
`getIntZerosAttr` is crashing when `op.dest().getType() == !firrtl.clock`
```c++
  auto replacement = op.src();
  if (srcValueOp) {
    // Replace with constant zero.
    if (isa<InvalidValueOp>(srcValueOp)) {
      auto constant =
          rewriter.create<ConstantOp>(op.dest().getLoc(), op.dest().getType(),
                                      getIntZerosAttr(op.dest().getType()));
      replacement = constant;
    }
```
The last update to this code is was here: https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/2288
